---
title: "Draft: minimal addition to the Discussion"
output: html_document
bibliography: REFERENCES.bib
---

<!--
Suggestion only. Not applied to visStatistics.Rmd. Two sentences,
proposed for the Discussion, to be inserted after the existing
"imperfect routing gates" paragraph (current line 836) and before the
Central Limit Theorem paragraph (current line 838).

Why here: lines 834/836 already frame the gate's imperfection as a
power/sample-size problem (the iid-sample view). The residual-object
problem — residuals are non-iid and supernormal by construction — is
not yet stated. These two sentences add it without restructuring.
Citations read in full: Cook & Weisberg 1982 p. 14; Das & Imon 2016 p. 10.
-->

## Proposed insertion (after current line 836)

> A further limitation is specific to residual-based gates: fitting the
> linear model renders the residuals heteroscedastic and mutually
> correlated by construction and pulls their distribution toward
> normality ("supernormality"), so a normality test applied to
> residuals tends to under-reject [@Cook:1982, p. 14; @Das:2016, p. 10].
> The diagnostic plots therefore remain the primary safeguard against
> this conservative bias.

# References {.unnumbered}
